perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS, the ValueAct Group currently beneficially owns 19,125,204 shares of the common stock, par value $0.01 per share, of the Company (the "Common Stock"), which represents approximately 10.77% of the issued and outstanding shares of Common Stock.
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS, the ValueAct Group currently beneficially owns 19,125,204 shares of the common stock, par value $0.01 per share, of the Company (the "Common Stock"), which represents approximately 5% of the issued and outstanding shares of Common Stock.
perturbation.explanation: The percentage of shares owned by ValueAct Group is modified to create a contradiction. This directly contradicts Section 1(f)(i), which states the agreement terminates if the ValueAct Group owns less than 7.5% of outstanding Common Stock. Changing the initial ownership percentage creates an immediate conflict, as the agreement is predicated on an ownership level that, according to the modification, does not exist.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (f) The Company's obligations hereunder shall terminate immediately, and the ValueAct Designee shall promptly offer to resign from the Board, and any committee of the Board on which he then sits (and, if requested by the Company, promptly deliver his written resignation to the Board (which shall provide for his immediate resignation) it being understood that it shall be in the Board's sole discretion whether to accept or reject such resignation) if: (i) members of the ValueAct Group, collectively, cease to beneficially own at least 7.5% of the Company's outstanding Common Stock; (ii) the ValueAct Designee ceases to satisfy the conditions set forth in clauses (c)-(d) above; (iii) a member of the ValueAct Group, including the ValueAct Designee, otherwise ceases to comply or breaches any of the terms of this Agreement or the Confidentiality Agreement; or (iv) the employment of the ValueAct Designee with the ValueAct Group is terminated for any reason. The ValueAct Group agrees to cause the ValueAct Designee to resign from the Board if the ValueAct Designee fails to resign if and when requested pursuant to this Section 1(f). Notwithstanding the foregoing, in the event of the occurrence of an event set forth in subsection (ii) or (iv) above, the provisions of Section 1(e) must be complied with before the Company's obligations hereunder may terminate.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the percentage of shares owned by ValueAct Group from approximately 10.77% to approximately 5%. This directly contradicts Section 1(f)(i), which states that the agreement terminates if the ValueAct Group owns less than 7.5% of the Company's outstanding Common Stock. The modified text indicates ownership below this threshold, creating a direct conflict that triggers the termination clause. Therefore, the change introduces a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Subject to the satisfactory completion of the Company's customary background check, the Board shall appoint the ValueAct Designee to serve as a director beginning within ten (10) calendar days following receipt of the ValueAct Group's written request to the Company to have the ValueAct Designee appointed to the Board; provided that, at such time, the Board does not conclude in good faith and based on the written opinion of outside legal counsel that such appointment would constitute a breach of the directors' fiduciary duties.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to the satisfactory completion of the Company's customary background check, the Board may, at its sole discretion, appoint the ValueAct Designee to serve as a director beginning within ten (10) calendar days following receipt of the ValueAct Group's written request to the Company to have the ValueAct Designee appointed to the Board; provided that, at such time, the Board does not conclude in good faith and based on the written opinion of outside legal counsel that such appointment would constitute a breach of the directors' fiduciary duties.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'shall appoint' to 'may, at its sole discretion, appoint' introduces uncertainty. This contradicts the spirit and intent of the Cooperation Agreement. While the original clause suggests a commitment to appoint the ValueAct Designee subject to certain conditions, the revised clause makes the appointment discretionary, thereby undermining the core agreement. This change creates conflict and could be used to avoid the commitment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS, the Board has determined that it is in the best interests of the Company to appoint the ValueAct Designee on the terms set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the board 'shall appoint' the ValueAct Designee, indicating an obligation. The changed text replaces 'shall appoint' with 'may, at its sole discretion, appoint,' making the appointment discretionary. This directly contradicts the 'WHEREAS' clause in the contradicted text, which states that 'the Board has determined that it is in the best interests of the Company to appoint the ValueAct Designee,' thereby creating a strong in-text contradiction. The modified text allows the board to not appoint the ValueAct Designee, even though the contradicted text asserts that the board has already determined it is in the company's best interest to make the appointment. This falls under the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' because the changed clause alters the obligations and undermines the core agreement.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement is effective as of the date hereof and shall remain in full force and effect for the period (the "Covered Period") commencing on the date hereof and ending on the date that is the earliest of: (i) the Company's failure to appoint the ValueAct Designee to the Board following the ValueAct Group's written request to the Company to have the ValueAct Designee appointed to the Board pursuant to Section 1(a) of this Agreement; (ii) the failure of the Company to comply in good faith with Section 1(e) of this Agreement; or (iii) the date which is the 60t h day prior to the Company's 2016 annual meeting of stockholders.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement is effective as of the date hereof and shall remain in full force and effect for the period (the "Covered Period") commencing on the date hereof and ending at any time, at the company's discretion.
perturbation.explanation: By modifying the termination conditions to 'ending at any time, at the company's discretion,' there is now an In-Text Contradiction against the intended meaning of this Agreement, as this Cooperation Agreement looks to guarantee the ValueAct Designee appointment into the Board.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS, the Board has determined that it is in the best interests of the Company to appoint the ValueAct Designee on the terms set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the termination conditions to allow the company to terminate the agreement 'at any time, at the company's discretion.' This directly contradicts the 'WHEREAS' clause, which states that the Board has determined that it is in the best interests of the Company to appoint the ValueAct Designee on the terms set forth in this Agreement. By granting the company unilateral termination power, the changed text undermines the agreement's purpose of guaranteeing the ValueAct Designee appointment and creates a direct conflict with the stated intention in the 'WHEREAS' clause, which is a strong in-text contradiction based on the provided information.
